text.title: SDLC + AI Domain Overview Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the sdlc-ai methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Domain MUST partition into 10 canonical clusters: lang / lint / test / tracker / kb / task / mr / inc / sec / gov.
Routing MUST start from a concrete task signal (file extension, tool name, lifecycle stage), never from prose intent.
rule.rationale: Vague routing matches the wrong cluster.

rule.statement: Router MUST return at most 3 candidate methodologies; further narrowing belongs to the leaf.
Each cluster description MUST cite a representative methodology with stable slug.
Routing MUST flag clusters where >50% methodologies are draft as `cluster_status: incomplete`.
Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
